extra.reasoning:
Property: never (rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b) jointly true.

rec_drug_a is set true ONLY by r5 (priority 50), whose condition is (asthma AND qt_prolongation AND g6pd_deficiency AND pregnancy). No other rule touches rec_drug_a, and nothing sets it false except the default. So rec_drug_a = true requires exactly that 4-way conjunction to be true.

rec_drug_b is affected by two rules: r1 (priority 50, IF asthma THEN true) and r4 (priority 90, IF the same 4-way conjunction THEN false). Resolution is by highest priority per output.

Consider any input where rec_drug_a = true. That forces the 4-way conjunction (asthma, qt_prolongation, g6pd_deficiency, pregnancy) all true. Under that input, both r1 and r4 match rec_drug_b. r4 has priority 90 > r1's priority 50, so r4 wins and rec_drug_b := false. Therefore whenever rec_drug_a is true, rec_drug_b is necessarily false.

Conversely, rec_drug_b can only be true when asthma is true but the full 4-way conjunction is NOT satisfied (so r4 does not fire); in that case r5 does not fire either, so rec_drug_a remains false (default). 

There is no reachable input assignment with rec_drug_a = true and rec_drug_b = true. The higher-priority r4 (90) deliberately suppresses drug b exactly in the regime where drug a is recommended. The property holds over the entire input space.
